title: Sweet cheese strudel
subtitle: Pulled strudel dough filled with vanilla-scented ricotta and raisins
excerpt: Sweet cheese strudel is a Central European pastry: filo-thin pulled dough wrapped around a filling of ricotta, vanilla and raisins. Making the strudel dough from scratch and pulling it on a large cloth to paper-thin is the advanced part. Filo pastry is the practical substitute but the flavour and texture are noticeably different.
sourceNotes: Sweet cheese strudel (Topfenstrudel or Quarkstrudel) is an Austrian and Hungarian pastry tradition documented in Central European baking texts from the nineteenth century. The pulled strudel dough technique predates the use of commercially available filo pastry and remains the traditional method in Austrian and Czech baking. The ricotta-and-raisin filling reflects the Italian influence on Central European pastry. This recipe is synthesised from the established Central European strudel tradition.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: The strudel dough must rest for 30 minutes before pulling. The filling can be made up to a day ahead and refrigerated.

glossaryTerms.definition: Strudel dough is pulled rather than rolled. After resting for 30 minutes, the soft dough is draped over the back of your hands and gently stretched from the centre outward, moving around the dough repeatedly until it is translucent and thin enough to read through. The cloth method uses a large table covered in a lightly floured cloth; the dough is laid on the cloth and pulled from underneath by sliding your hands under it, palms facing down. Any holes that form during pulling can be ignored; they will be covered by the roll.

Supermarket ricotta is often wetter than the Italian original. For a strudel filling that holds together and does not make the pastry soggy, drain the ricotta in a fine-mesh sieve for 30 minutes before using. The drained ricotta should hold its shape when scooped rather than pooling.

content.text: The skill in this recipe is

. It requires practice. The dough must rest for a full 30 minutes after mixing so the gluten relaxes; attempting to pull it too soon results in tearing. Beginners can substitute a double layer of filo pastry brushed with melted butter; the result is crunchier and less delicate, but the recipe still works.
before making the filling. Wet filling turns the pastry base soggy before it can bake through.

items.prepNote: drained in a sieve for 30 minutes

content.text: Mix the 200 g flour and half a teaspoon salt in a bowl. Add the 2 tbsp vegetable oil and 100 ml warm water and mix to a soft, smooth dough. Knead for 5 minutes until it is elastic and barely sticky. Brush the dough with a little oil, wrap in cling film and leave to rest at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Beat together the 400 g drained ricotta, 100 g cream cheese, 60 g caster sugar, 2 egg yolks, 1 tsp vanilla and lemon zest until smooth. Stir in the 80 g raisins.

Heat the oven to 190 degrees C. Cover a large table with a clean cloth and dust with flour. Place the dough in the centre and begin rolling it thin, then continue pulling by hand from underneath until it is as thin as possible. Brush the surface with melted butter, leaving a 5 cm border at one long edge. Spread the filling in a 10 cm-wide log about 5 cm from the near long edge. Using the cloth to help, roll the strudel away from you over the filling, folding in the short ends as you go. Ease onto a lined baking tray, seam-side down, and brush all over with melted butter. Bake for 30 to 35 minutes until the pastry is deep brown and sounds hollow when tapped. Dust with icing sugar and leave to cool for 15 minutes before slicing.

items.solution: The dough was not rested long enough or was pulled too aggressively. Wrap it and rest for a further 15 minutes. Pull from the underside with flat hands, moving slowly and progressively from the centre outward rather than from the edges inward.
The ricotta was not drained well. Next time drain in a fine sieve for at least 30 minutes and squeeze gently in a clean cloth. Also, ensure the baking tray was properly preheated.
The short ends were not folded and sealed tightly during rolling. Leave a 5 cm gap at each short end when spreading the filling, and fold those ends in over the filling before rolling up.
